mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic momentum that lifts equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle with inflation above 2.0 percent
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the slope coefficient of FEDFUNDS on forward returns becomes positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures and credit balances indicate expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply-side capacity expansion that reduces input cost inflation and boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and growth in nonfarm payrolls confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that sustains consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a directional accuracy below 40% over a 4-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts peak and credit growth decelerates, equity markets price in imminent earnings slowdowns, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating credit growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of late-cycle deceleration episodes, or the rolling correlation between HOUST deceleration and SPX returns becomes positive.
